title: narcissus patulus var. tineoi
intro: Narcissus patulus var. tineoi, also known as the Spanish bluebells, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant's bulbs and flowers are used to treat a variety of health issues, such as respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, and are also used to reduce stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, Narcissus patulus var. tineoi is often used in teas, infusions, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
